tasks.title: Initial Git Commit
tasks.description: Create the first commit for the agent-reviews repository with all current files using conventional commit format.
tasks.details:
Execute the following steps:

1. Review all files staged for commit (current untracked files):
   - .env.example, .gitignore, .vscodeignore
   - README.md, package.json, package-lock.json, tsconfig.json
   - src/*.ts (extension.ts, comments.ts, parser.ts, resolver.ts, icons.ts)
   - icons/*.svg (blocking, important, suggestion, nit, praise, learning)
   - .taskmaster/ directory if needed

2. Ensure .gitignore includes:
   - node_modules/
   - out/
   - *.vsix
   - .reviews/

3. Create commit with conventional format:
   ```bash
   git add .
   git commit -m "feat: initial VSCode extension for AI code review comments
   
   - Inline display of review comments using VSCode Comments API
   - Support for review JSON files from .reviews/ directory
   - Git submodule path resolution via .gitmodules parsing
   - Severity-based icons (blocking, important, suggestion, nit, praise, learning)
   - File watching with auto-refresh on JSON changes
   - Interactive actions: edit, change severity, delete comments
   - Commands: Load Review File, Reload Comments, Clear All Comments"
   ```
tasks.testStrategy: Verify commit was created successfully with `git log --oneline -1`. Check all intended files are included with `git show --stat HEAD`. Ensure no sensitive files (node_modules, .env) are committed.

tasks.title: Fix Edit/Save Flow
tasks.description: Verify and fix the edit flow (pencil icon -> edit text -> save) to ensure it correctly persists updated comment body to the JSON file.
tasks.details:
The current implementation in comments.ts and extension.ts has the edit flow wired up. Verify and fix:

1. **Edit initiation (setCommentEditing in comments.ts:101-106)**:
   - Sets `comment.mode = vscode.CommentMode.Editing`
   - Sets `comment.contextValue = 'agentReviewEditing'` to show save/cancel buttons
   - Triggers UI refresh with `thread.comments = [...thread.comments]`

2. **Save command (agentReview.saveComment in extension.ts:197-212)**:
   - Currently reads `comment.body` which VSCode updates during editing
   - Issue: When in editing mode, VSCode provides the new text via `comment.body` but we need to verify this is a string or MarkdownString
   - Fix: Extract text properly:
     ```typescript
     const newBody = typeof comment.body === 'string' 
       ? comment.body 
       : (comment.body as vscode.MarkdownString).value;
     ```

3. **Persistence (updateCommentBody in comments.ts:71-84)**:
   - Updates `this.review.comments[comment.commentIndex].body = newBody`
   - Calls `persistAndRebuild()` which saves and rebuilds threads
   - The `isSaving` flag prevents file watcher from reloading during save

4. **Potential issues to investigate**:
   - Verify the comment index mapping survives thread rebuilds
   - Ensure MarkdownString body is properly converted to plain string for JSON storage
   - Check that the editing mode is cleared after save (mode should return to Preview)

5. **Fix required in updateCommentBody**:
   ```typescript
   async updateCommentBody(comment: AgentReviewComment, newBody: string): Promise<void> {
     if (!this.review || !this.reviewFilePath) return;
     const source = this.review.comments[comment.commentIndex];
     if (source) {
       // Store plain string, not MarkdownString
       source.body = newBody;
     }
     await this.persistAndRebuild();
   }
   ```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Load a review file with comments
2. Click edit (pencil) on a comment
3. Modify the text
4. Click save (checkmark)
5. Verify: Comment displays updated text
6. Check .reviews/*.json file: body should contain new text
7. Reload extension: comment should still show updated text
8. Test cancel button: edit then cancel, verify original text restored

tasks.title: Add GitHub Posting Command
tasks.description: Implement agentReview.postToGitHub command that posts all comments from the current review to the GitHub PR as a single review using the gh CLI.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Ensure gh CLI is installed and authenticated (`gh auth status`)
2. Load a review file for a real PR
3. Run 'Agent Review: Post to GitHub PR' command
4. Verify confirmation dialog appears
5. Confirm and check progress notification
6. Verify on GitHub: review appears with correct verdict, overview as body, and inline comments at correct file:line positions
7. Test error cases: no gh CLI, not authenticated, invalid PR number

tasks.title: Add Status Bar Item
tasks.description: Show a status bar item when a review is loaded indicating the PR number, verdict, and comment count. Clicking should reload comments.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Open workspace without review file - status bar should not appear
2. Load a review file - status bar should show PR #, verdict icon, comment count
3. Hover over status bar - tooltip shows PR title, verdict, count, and overview
4. Click status bar - comments should reload
5. Clear all comments - status bar should hide
6. Load review with different verdicts (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT) - verify correct icon displays

tasks.title: Package as Installable VSIX
tasks.description: Add @vscode/vsce as a dev dependency with a package script. Document the install flow and test the packaged extension.
tasks.details:
Set up VSIX packaging for distribution:

1. **Update package.json** - add vsce and packaging scripts:
   ```json
   {
     "scripts": {
       "compile": "tsc -p ./",
       "watch": "tsc -watch -p ./",
       "package": "npm run compile && vsce package",
       "vscode:prepublish": "npm run compile"
     },
     "devDependencies": {
       "@types/node": "^20.0.0",
       "@types/vscode": "^1.85.0",
       "@vscode/vsce": "^3.0.0",
       "typescript": "^5.3.0"
     }
   }
   ```

2. **Verify .vscodeignore** includes (already present, verify):
   ```
   src/
   tsconfig.json
   .gitignore
   node_modules/
   .taskmaster/
   *.md
   ```

3. **Ensure package.json has required fields**:
   - `publisher`: Already set to "smallcase"
   - `repository`: Add if missing for marketplace
   - `license`: Add "MIT" or appropriate license
   - `icon`: Optional - add an extension icon

4. **Update README.md installation section** (already documented):
   ```bash
   # From the extension directory
   npm run package
   code --install-extension agent-review-0.1.0.vsix
   ```

5. **Add repository field** (optional but recommended):
   ```json
   "repository": {
     "type": "git",
     "url": "https://github.com/smallcase/agent-reviews.git"
   }
   ```

6. **Run packaging**:
   ```bash
   npm install
   npm run package
   ```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run `npm run package` - should create agent-review-0.1.0.vsix
2. Open a fresh VSCode window (not Extension Development Host)
3. Install: `code --install-extension agent-review-0.1.0.vsix`
4. Open a workspace with .reviews/ directory containing a review file
5. Verify extension activates (check Extension Host output)
6. Verify commands appear in Command Palette
7. Verify inline comments display correctly
8. Verify edit/delete/severity change actions work
9. Uninstall and reinstall to test clean installation

tasks.title: Update Code-Review-Excellence Skill Output Path
tasks.description: Document how to update the Claude Code /code-review-excellence skill to save review files directly to the target project's .reviews/ directory.
tasks.details:
The /code-review-excellence skill currently saves to /tmp/. Document how users can configure it:

1. **Add section to README.md**:
   ```markdown
   ## Integrating with Claude Code's /code-review-excellence skill

   By default, the skill saves review files to `/tmp/`. To have reviews automatically loaded:

   ### Option 1: Move the file after generation
   After running `/code-review-excellence` on a PR, move the generated file:
   ```bash
   mv /tmp/pr-<number>-review-comments.json .reviews/
   ```

   ### Option 2: Configure the skill output path
   If you have access to customize the skill, update the output path:
   ```typescript
   const outputPath = path.join(projectRoot, '.reviews', `pr-${prNumber}-review-comments.json`);
   ```

   ### Option 3: Use a symlink (development)
   ```bash
   ln -s /tmp/pr-*-review-comments.json .reviews/
   ```
   ```

2. **If skill source is accessible**, the recommended change is:
   - Detect if `.reviews/` directory exists in the workspace
   - If yes, save directly there
   - If no, fall back to `/tmp/` with a message suggesting to create `.reviews/`

3. **Add helper command** (optional enhancement):
   ```typescript
   // In extension.ts - add command to import from /tmp/
   context.subscriptions.push(
     vscode.commands.registerCommand('agentReview.importFromTmp', async () => {
       const tmpFiles = await vscode.workspace.findFiles('/tmp/pr-*-review-comments.json');
       // Show picker, copy selected file to .reviews/
     })
   );
   ```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run /code-review-excellence skill on a test PR
2. Note where file is saved (should be /tmp/)
3. Follow documented steps to move file to .reviews/
4. Verify extension picks up the file automatically
5. If skill can be modified: update output path, verify direct save to .reviews/

tasks.title: Add TypeScript Strict Null Checks
tasks.description: Enable strictNullChecks in tsconfig.json and fix any resulting type errors to improve code robustness.
tasks.details:
The tsconfig.json has `strict: true` which includes strictNullChecks. Verify all code handles nullable types properly:

1. **Audit nullable returns** - Functions that may return undefined:
   - `getWorkspaceRoot()`: returns `string | undefined`
   - `getReviewsDir()`: returns `string | undefined`
   - `resolveCommentUri()`: returns `vscode.Uri | undefined`

2. **Check array access** - Ensure bounds checking:
   - `this.review.comments[comment.commentIndex]` - verify index validity
   - `entries[0]` - verify array not empty

3. **Fix potential issues in comments.ts**:
   ```typescript
   // Line 79-82 - add null check
   const source = this.review.comments[comment.commentIndex];
   if (!source) {
     console.warn(`Comment index ${comment.commentIndex} not found`);
     return;
   }
   source.body = newBody;
   ```

4. **Fix extension.ts watcher callback**:
   ```typescript
   // Line 116 - filename already null-checked, but TypeScript may not narrow
   const changedFile = path.join(reviewsDir, filename); // Remove !
   ```

5. **Run compilation** to catch any remaining issues:
   ```bash
   npm run compile
   ```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run `npm run compile` with no errors
2. Test edge cases: load empty review file (no comments)
3. Test with review file where comment has invalid index
4. Test with workspace that has no .reviews/ directory
5. Test file watcher with rapid file changes

tasks.title: Add Error Recovery for Corrupted JSON
tasks.description: Improve error handling when review JSON files are malformed or corrupted during editing.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Create a malformed JSON file (missing closing brace) - should show clear error
2. Create review with missing 'pr' field - should show specific error
3. Create review with comment missing 'body' - should show index of bad comment
4. Save valid review, verify file is valid JSON
5. Test 'Open File' action in error message

tasks.title: Add Multi-Review Support
tasks.description: Allow loading multiple review files simultaneously and switching between them via quick pick.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Place multiple review files in .reviews/ directory
2. Load first review - verify comments appear
3. Run 'Switch Review' command - verify picker shows all reviews
4. Select different review - verify comments switch
5. Status bar should update to show new review
6. Previous review's comments should be cleared

tasks.title: Add Keyboard Shortcuts
tasks.description: Add keyboard shortcuts for common actions like reload, clear, and post to GitHub.
tasks.details:
Add keybindings for frequently used commands:

1. **Add keybindings to package.json**:
   ```json
   "contributes": {
     "keybindings": [
       {
         "command": "agentReview.reload",
         "key": "ctrl+shift+r",
         "mac": "cmd+shift+r",
         "when": "editorTextFocus"
       },
       {
         "command": "agentReview.clear",
         "key": "ctrl+shift+c",
         "mac": "cmd+shift+c",
         "when": "editorTextFocus"
       },
       {
         "command": "agentReview.postToGitHub",
         "key": "ctrl+shift+g",
         "mac": "cmd+shift+g",
         "when": "editorTextFocus"
       },
       {
         "command": "agentReview.loadFile",
         "key": "ctrl+shift+l",
         "mac": "cmd+shift+l"
       }
     ]
   }
   ```

2. **Add when clause context** for better keybinding scoping:
   ```typescript
   // In loadComments, set context when review is loaded
   vscode.commands.executeCommand(
     'setContext', 
     'agentReview.hasReview', 
     true
   );
   
   // In clear command
   vscode.commands.executeCommand(
     'setContext', 
     'agentReview.hasReview', 
     false
   );
   ```

3. **Update keybindings to use context**:
   ```json
   {
     "command": "agentReview.reload",
     "key": "ctrl+shift+r",
     "mac": "cmd+shift+r",
     "when": "agentReview.hasReview"
   }
   ```

4. **Document shortcuts in README**:
   ```markdown
   ## Keyboard Shortcuts
   
   | Command | Windows/Linux | Mac |
   |---------|---------------|-----|
   | Reload Comments | Ctrl+Shift+R | Cmd+Shift+R |
   | Clear All | Ctrl+Shift+C | Cmd+Shift+C |
   | Post to GitHub | Ctrl+Shift+G | Cmd+Shift+G |
   | Load Review File | Ctrl+Shift+L | Cmd+Shift+L |
   ```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Load a review file
2. Press Cmd+Shift+R (Mac) / Ctrl+Shift+R (Windows) - should reload
3. Press Cmd+Shift+C - should clear comments
4. Load review again, press Cmd+Shift+G - should prompt to post to GitHub
5. With no review loaded, shortcuts for reload/clear/post should not trigger
6. Cmd+Shift+L should always open file picker

tasks.title: Update File Naming Convention to Include Repo Name
tasks.description: Change the review file naming convention from `pr-<number>-review-comments.json` to `<repo-slug>-pr-<number>-review-comments.json` to support multi-repo workspaces and monorepos with submodules.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Backwards compatibility with old format:**
   - Place a file named `pr-58-review-comments.json` in `.reviews/`
   - Open workspace - extension should activate and load the review
   - Verify `findReviewFiles()` returns the old-format file

2. **New format detection:**
   - Place a file named `backend-api-pr-58-review-comments.json` in `.reviews/`
   - Open workspace - extension should activate and load the review
   - Verify file watcher detects changes to new-format files

3. **Multi-file scenario:**
   - Place both `pr-58-review-comments.json` and `backend-api-pr-58-review-comments.json` in `.reviews/`
   - Verify both appear in "Switch Review" quick pick
   - Verify each can be loaded independently

4. **Repo slug generation:**
   - Test `getRepoSlug("acme/backend-api")` returns `"backend-api"`
   - Test `getRepoSlug("acme/frontend_app")` returns `"frontend-app"`
   - Test `getRepoSlug("my.service")` returns `"my-service"`
   - Test `getRepoSlug("acme/UPPER-case")` returns `"upper-case"`

5. **Activation event:**
   - In a fresh workspace, create `.reviews/myrepo-pr-1-review-comments.json`
   - Verify extension activates automatically (check Extension Host output)

6. **File watcher:**
   - Modify a new-format review file externally
   - Verify comments auto-refresh after the debounce delay

tasks.title: Create JSON Schema File for Review Format Validation
tasks.description: Create a JSON Schema file (schema.json) at the repository root that formally defines the review JSON structure using JSON Schema 2020-12 specification, enabling AI agents to validate their output and IDEs to provide autocomplete for review files.
tasks.details:
Create `/schema.json` at the repository root with a complete JSON Schema 2020-12 definition that mirrors the TypeScript interfaces in `src/parser.ts`.

**1. Schema file structure:**
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://github.com/smallcase/agent-reviews/schema.json",
  "title": "Agent Review File",
  "description": "Schema for AI-generated code review comments",
  "type": "object",
  ...
}
```

**2. Define all types from `src/parser.ts` (lines 4-42):**

- **Severity enum** (line 4-10):
  ```json
  "$defs": {
    "Severity": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": ["blocking", "important", "suggestion", "nit", "praise", "learning"],
      "description": "Comment severity level"
    }
  }
  ```

- **ReviewAuthor** (lines 12-15):
  ```json
  "ReviewAuthor": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "name": { "type": "string", "description": "Display name (e.g., Claude Opus 4)" },
      "iconUrl": { "type": "string", "format": "uri", "description": "URL to avatar image" }
    },
    "required": ["name"],
    "additionalProperties": false
  }
  ```

- **ReviewComment** (lines 17-25):
  ```json
  "ReviewComment": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "path": { "type": "string", "description": "File path relative to repo root" },
      "line": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "description": "Line number (1-based)" },
      "side": { "type": "string", "enum": ["LEFT", "RIGHT"], "description": "LEFT (old) or RIGHT (new) code" },
      "severity": { "$ref": "#/$defs/Severity" },
      "body": { "type": "string", "description": "Comment text in Markdown" },
      "timestamp": { "type": "string", "format": "date-time", "description": "ISO 8601 timestamp" },
      "author": { "$ref": "#/$defs/ReviewAuthor" }
    },
    "required": ["path", "line", "side", "severity", "body"],
    "additionalProperties": false
  }
  ```

- **ReviewFile** (lines 27-42) - the root schema object with:
  - `pr` object with repo, number, title, base, head (all required)
  - `author` (optional ReviewAuthor)
  - `summary` object with verdict, overview, strengths (all required)
  - `comments` array of ReviewComment

**3. Add metadata for IDE autocomplete:**
- Include `"description"` for every property
- Use `"examples"` arrays for complex fields
- Add `"default"` values where sensible

**4. Cross-reference with README.md documentation:**
- Ensure verdict values match: `APPROVE`, `REQUEST_CHANGES`, `COMMENT`
- Ensure severity values match the table in README (lines 167-174)
- Ensure side values are `LEFT` and `RIGHT` as documented

**5. Update README.md to reference the schema:**
Add a note in the "Review JSON Schema" section mentioning:
```markdown
For IDE autocomplete and validation, add to your review JSON:
`"$schema": "./path/to/agent-reviews/schema.json"`
```

**6. Consider adding to package.json (optional enhancement):**
Could add a VS Code JSON schema association for `.reviews/*.json` files if desired.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Schema syntax validation:**
   - Use a JSON Schema validator (e.g., `ajv` CLI or online validator) to verify schema.json is valid JSON Schema 2020-12
   - Run: `npx ajv validate -s schema.json --spec=draft2020` (requires ajv-cli)

2. **Validate example from README:**
   - Extract the example JSON from README.md (lines 77-121)
   - Validate it against schema.json - should pass with no errors
   - Command: `npx ajv validate -s schema.json -d example.json`

3. **Test required field validation:**
   - Create a review JSON missing `pr.number` - should fail validation
   - Create a review JSON missing `comments[0].body` - should fail validation
   - Create a review JSON missing `summary.verdict` - should fail validation

4. **Test enum validation:**
   - Create a review with invalid severity `"critical"` - should fail
   - Create a review with invalid side `"BOTH"` - should fail
   - Create a review with invalid verdict `"REJECT"` - should fail

5. **Test optional fields:**
   - Create valid review without `author` - should pass
   - Create valid review without `timestamp` on comments - should pass
   - Create valid review without `iconUrl` on author - should pass

6. **IDE autocomplete verification:**
   - Add `"$schema": "./schema.json"` to a test review file
   - Open in VS Code - verify autocomplete suggests property names
   - Verify hover documentation appears for properties

7. **TypeScript consistency check:**
   - Review that all properties in schema.json match the interfaces in `src/parser.ts`
   - Verify required fields match: `path`, `line`, `side`, `severity`, `body` for comments
   - Verify optional fields: `timestamp`, `author`

tasks.title: Create CLAUDE.md Documentation for AI Agents
tasks.description: Create a CLAUDE.md file at the repository root containing instructions for AI agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) on how to generate review files for the agent-reviews VSCode extension, including file naming conventions, placement, schema reference, and templates.
tasks.details:
Create `/CLAUDE.md` at the repository root as a machine-readable documentation file for AI coding assistants.

**1. File structure overview:**
```markdown
# Agent Reviews - AI Agent Instructions

## Quick Reference
- **File location:** `.reviews/` directory at workspace root
- **File naming:** `<repo-slug>-pr-<number>-review-comments.json` (or legacy `pr-<number>-review-comments.json`)
- **Schema:** Reference `/schema.json` for validation

## File Naming Convention
... detailed rules ...

## Minimal Template
... minimal valid JSON ...

## Full Template
... complete JSON with all fields ...
```

**2. Document the file naming convention in detail:**
Based on Task #11 (Update File Naming Convention) and current `src/parser.ts` (lines 76-89), document:
- **Preferred format:** `<repo-slug>-pr-<number>-review-comments.json`
- **Legacy format:** `pr-<number>-review-comments.json` (backwards compatible)
- **Repo slug derivation:** From `pr.repo` field - take repo name after `/`, lowercase, replace special chars with hyphens
- Examples table:
  | `pr.repo` | Expected filename |
  |-----------|-------------------|
  | `acme/backend-api` | `backend-api-pr-142-review-comments.json` |
  | `smallcase/frontend_app` | `frontend-app-pr-58-review-comments.json` |

**3. Document file placement:**
- Must be placed in `.reviews/` directory at workspace root
- The `.reviews/` directory should be gitignored
- Extension watches this directory and auto-loads on file creation/change
- Reference `package.json` line 14 for activation event pattern

**4. Include schema reference section:**
- Point to `/schema.json` (from Task #12) for formal JSON Schema 2020-12 definition
- Show how to add `$schema` property for IDE autocomplete:
  ```json
  {
    "$schema": "../path/to/agent-reviews/schema.json",
    ...
  }
  ```
- Reference TypeScript interfaces in `src/parser.ts` lines 4-42

**5. Provide minimal template (required fields only):**
Based on validation in `src/parser.ts` (lines 48-52, 54-64), the minimal valid review needs:
```json
{
  "pr": {
    "repo": "org/repo-name",
    "number": 123,
    "title": "PR title",
    "base": "main",
    "head": "feature-branch"
  },
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "COMMENT",
    "overview": "Brief summary of the review.",
    "strengths": []
  },
  "comments": [
    {
      "path": "src/file.ts",
      "line": 42,
      "side": "RIGHT",
      "severity": "suggestion",
      "body": "Comment text in Markdown."
    }
  ]
}
```

**6. Provide full template with all optional fields:**
Based on README.md example (lines 77-121) and `src/parser.ts` interfaces:
```json
{
  "pr": {
    "repo": "org/repo-name",
    "number": 142,
    "title": "Fix: descriptive PR title",
    "base": "main",
    "head": "fix/branch-name"
  },
  "author": {
    "name": "Claude Opus 4",
    "iconUrl": "https://example.com/avatar.png"
  },
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "REQUEST_CHANGES",
    "overview": "One paragraph summary of the review findings.",
    "strengths": [
      "First strength noted",
      "Second strength noted"
    ]
  },
  "comments": [
    {
      "path": "src/handlers/users.ts",
      "line": 42,
      "side": "RIGHT",
      "severity": "blocking",
      "body": "**Issue:** Description of the problem.\n\n**Suggestion:** How to fix it.",
      "timestamp": "2026-03-25T10:30:00Z",
      "author": {
        "name": "Specific Agent Name",
        "iconUrl": "https://example.com/other-avatar.png"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

**7. Document field values and enums:**
Reference README.md (lines 125-174) and `src/parser.ts` (lines 4-10):
- **verdict:** `APPROVE`, `REQUEST_CHANGES`, `COMMENT`
- **severity:** `blocking`, `important`, `suggestion`, `nit`, `praise`, `learning`
- **side:** `RIGHT` (new/added code), `LEFT` (old/deleted code)
- Include brief descriptions of when to use each severity level

**8. Document path resolution for monorepos:**
Reference README.md (lines 186-194) and `src/resolver.ts`:
- For monorepos with git submodules, the extension resolves paths via `.gitmodules`
- Comment `path` should be relative to the repo root (matching GitHub PR diff paths)
- Example: If reviewing `acme/backend-api` which is a submodule at `services/backend/`, the comment path `src/handlers/users.ts` resolves to `services/backend/src/handlers/users.ts`

**9. Add tips for AI agents:**
- Always include `pr.repo` matching the GitHub repository being reviewed
- Use `RIGHT` side for comments on added/modified lines (most common)
- Use `LEFT` side only for comments on deleted lines
- Keep `body` content in Markdown format
- Severity guidelines: use `blocking` sparingly (must-fix), `suggestion` for alternatives, `nit` for style/minor issues
- Include `timestamp` in ISO 8601 format for traceability
- The `author` field is optional - falls back to default "Agent Review" with severity icon
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **File existence and location:**
   - Verify `CLAUDE.md` exists at repository root (same level as `package.json`)
   - Verify file is not in any subdirectory

2. **Minimal template validation:**
   - Copy the minimal template from CLAUDE.md to `.reviews/test-pr-1-review-comments.json`
   - Open workspace in VSCode with the agent-review extension
   - Verify extension activates and loads the minimal review without errors
   - Verify the comment appears at the correct file/line

3. **Full template validation:**
   - Copy the full template from CLAUDE.md to `.reviews/repo-name-pr-2-review-comments.json`
   - Verify extension loads all fields correctly
   - Verify author information displays in comment header
   - Verify timestamp appears in comment
   - Verify all severity types render with correct icons

4. **Schema reference accuracy:**
   - Ensure the schema path mentioned in CLAUDE.md matches the actual schema.json location
   - Validate both templates against schema.json:
     ```bash
     npx ajv validate -s schema.json -d minimal-template.json
     npx ajv validate -s schema.json -d full-template.json
     ```

5. **File naming convention documentation:**
   - Verify documented patterns match regex in `src/parser.ts` `findReviewFiles()` function
   - Verify examples in CLAUDE.md match the pattern `<repo-slug>-pr-<number>-review-comments.json`
   - Test both legacy (`pr-*`) and new (`<slug>-pr-*`) formats are mentioned

6. **Field documentation completeness:**
   - Cross-reference all required fields in CLAUDE.md with `src/parser.ts` interfaces (lines 4-42)
   - Verify verdict enum values match README.md (lines 135, 179-184)
   - Verify severity enum values match `src/parser.ts` (lines 4-10)

7. **AI agent usability:**
   - Give CLAUDE.md to an AI agent (Claude, Cursor, etc.) and ask it to generate a review file
   - Verify the generated file is valid and loads in the extension
   - Verify the agent correctly interprets the naming convention

tasks.title: Create Reusable Agent Prompt Template for Review Generation
tasks.description: Create a reusable prompt template at `.taskmaster/templates/review-prompt.md` that any AI agent can use as instructions to generate properly formatted review files for the agent-reviews VSCode extension, including inline JSON schema, file naming rules, severity guidelines with examples, and tone guidelines.
tasks.details:
Create `.taskmaster/templates/review-prompt.md` as a standalone, self-contained prompt template that AI agents can receive as instructions for generating code reviews.

**1. File Header and Purpose:**
```markdown
# Code Review Generation Instructions

You are performing a code review for a pull request. Generate a structured review file that can be loaded by the agent-reviews VSCode extension.

## Output Requirements
- Generate a single JSON file
- Follow the exact schema specified below
- Place observations as inline comments at specific file:line locations
```

**2. Include Inline JSON Schema (from Task #12's schema.json):**
Document the complete structure without requiring external file references:
```markdown
## JSON Schema

### Root Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `pr` | object | yes | Pull request metadata |
| `author` | object | no | Default author for all comments |
| `summary` | object | yes | Review summary |
| `comments` | array | yes | Array of inline review comments |

### PR Object
| Field | Type | Required |
|-------|------|----------|
| `pr.repo` | string | yes | GitHub repo in `org/repo` format |
| `pr.number` | number | yes | PR number |
| `pr.title` | string | yes | PR title |
| `pr.base` | string | yes | Base branch (e.g., `main`) |
| `pr.head` | string | yes | Head branch |

### Comment Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `path` | string | yes | File path relative to repo root |
| `line` | number | yes | Line number (1-based) |
| `side` | string | yes | `RIGHT` (new code) or `LEFT` (deleted code) |
| `severity` | string | yes | One of: `blocking`, `important`, `suggestion`, `nit`, `praise`, `learning` |
| `body` | string | yes | Comment text in Markdown |
| `timestamp` | string | no | ISO 8601 timestamp |
```

**3. File Naming Rules Section:**
Based on Task #11 conventions:
```markdown
## File Naming Convention

**Format:** `<repo-slug>-pr-<number>-review-comments.json`

**Repo slug derivation:**
1. Take the repository name from `pr.repo` (part after the `/`)
2. Convert to lowercase
3. Replace special characters (underscores, dots, etc.) with hyphens

**Examples:**
| `pr.repo` | Output filename |
|-----------|-----------------|
| `acme/backend-api` | `backend-api-pr-142-review-comments.json` |
| `acme/frontend_app` | `frontend-app-pr-58-review-comments.json` |
| `org/my.service.name` | `my-service-name-pr-99-review-comments.json` |

**Placement:** Save to `.reviews/` directory at workspace root.
```

**4. Severity Guidelines with Usage Examples:**
Based on README.md severity table and `src/icons.ts`:
```markdown
## Severity Levels

### `blocking` - Must Fix Before Merge
Use when the code has a defect that would cause bugs, security issues, or data loss.

**Example:**
```json
{
  "severity": "blocking",
  "body": "This SQL query concatenates user input directly, creating a SQL injection vulnerability. Should we use parameterized queries instead?"
}
```

### `important` - Should Fix, Discuss if Disagree
Use for significant issues that affect correctness, performance, or maintainability.

**Example:**
```json
{
  "severity": "important",
  "body": "This loop runs a database query on each iteration. Could we batch these into a single query to avoid N+1 performance issues?"
}
```

### `suggestion` - Alternative Approach to Consider
Use for optional improvements or different patterns worth considering.

**Example:**
```json
{
  "severity": "suggestion",
  "body": "This validation logic appears in three handlers. Would it make sense to extract it into a shared middleware function?"
}
```

### `nit` - Nice to Have, Not Blocking
Use for minor style, naming, or formatting observations.

**Example:**
```json
{
  "severity": "nit",
  "body": "The variable `data` could be more descriptive. Perhaps `userProfile` or `fetchedUser` would clarify its contents?"
}
```

### `praise` - Good Work, Keep It Up
Use to highlight well-written code that others can learn from.

**Example:**
```json
{
  "severity": "praise",
  "body": "Nice use of early returns here - it makes the happy path much clearer and reduces nesting."
}
```

### `learning` - Educational, No Action Needed
Use to explain concepts or provide context without requesting changes.

**Example:**
```json
{
  "severity": "learning",
  "body": "This uses the Null Object pattern - returning an empty array instead of null avoids the need for null checks in consumers."
}
```
```

**5. Tone Guidelines - Questioning Over Dictating:**
```markdown
## Tone Guidelines

### Question, Don't Command
Frame observations as questions rather than directives. This invites discussion and acknowledges you may lack context.

**Instead of:** "Fix this null check."
**Write:** "Should we add a null check here to handle the case where `user` is undefined?"

**Instead of:** "This is wrong."
**Write:** "Could this cause issues if the array is empty? What happens at line 45 in that case?"

**Instead of:** "Use async/await here."
**Write:** "Would async/await make this flow easier to follow than the current promise chain?"

### Explain the "Why"
Always include reasoning for your observation. Connect it to real consequences.

**Weak:** "This should be a constant."
**Better:** "This magic number appears in three places. Would extracting it to a named constant like `MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS` make future changes safer?"

### Acknowledge Uncertainty
If you're unsure about project conventions or context, say so.

**Example:** "I'm not sure if this project uses a specific error handling pattern - is throwing here consistent with how other handlers work?"

### Keep It Concise
One clear point per comment. If you have multiple observations about the same line, consider splitting them or prioritizing the most important one.
```

**6. Complete Example Output:**
Provide a full valid example showing all elements together:
```markdown
## Complete Example

```json
{
  "pr": {
    "repo": "acme/payment-service",
    "number": 287,
    "title": "Add retry logic for failed transactions",
    "base": "main",
    "head": "feature/transaction-retry"
  },
  "author": {
    "name": "Code Review Agent"
  },
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "REQUEST_CHANGES",
    "overview": "The retry logic implementation is solid, but there's a potential race condition in the transaction state update that should be addressed before merging.",
    "strengths": [
      "Good use of exponential backoff for retry delays",
      "Comprehensive error logging with transaction context",
      "Clear separation between retryable and non-retryable errors"
    ]
  },
  "comments": [
    {
      "path": "src/services/transaction.ts",
      "line": 142,
      "side": "RIGHT",
      "severity": "blocking",
      "body": "There's a race condition here: if two retry attempts run concurrently, both could read `status: 'pending'` and proceed. Should we add optimistic locking or a transaction-level mutex?"
    },
    {
      "path": "src/services/transaction.ts",
      "line": 156,
      "side": "RIGHT",
      "severity": "suggestion",
      "body": "The retry count is hardcoded to 3. Would it make sense to pull this from config so it can be tuned per environment?"
    },
    {
      "path": "src/services/transaction.ts",
      "line": 89,
      "side": "RIGHT",
      "severity": "praise",
      "body": "Nice implementation of exponential backoff with jitter - this will help prevent thundering herd issues during outages."
    }
  ]
}
```

**Filename:** `payment-service-pr-287-review-comments.json`
```

**7. Verdict Selection Guide:**
```markdown
## Verdict Guidelines

| Verdict | When to Use |
|---------|-------------|
| `APPROVE` | Code is ready to merge. Any comments are minor suggestions or praise. |
| `REQUEST_CHANGES` | There are blocking or important issues that should be addressed. |
| `COMMENT` | You have observations but aren't confident enough to approve or request changes. |
```

**8. Tips Section:**
```markdown
## Tips for Effective Reviews

1. **Focus on the diff** - Comment on changed lines, not unrelated existing code
2. **One comment per concern** - Don't bundle multiple issues in a single comment
3. **Be specific about location** - Use the exact line number where the issue occurs
4. **Use `RIGHT` for most comments** - `LEFT` is only for commenting on deleted code
5. **Include praise** - Recognize good patterns to reinforce them
6. **Timestamp is optional** - Include only if tracking review timing matters
```
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **File existence and location:**
   - Verify `.taskmaster/templates/review-prompt.md` exists at the correct path
   - Verify the file is readable and properly formatted Markdown

2. **Schema documentation completeness:**
   - Cross-reference all fields documented in the template against `src/parser.ts` interfaces (lines 4-42)
   - Verify required fields match: `path`, `line`, `side`, `severity`, `body` for comments
   - Verify `pr` object fields: `repo`, `number`, `title`, `base`, `head` (all required)
   - Verify `summary` fields: `verdict`, `overview`, `strengths` (all required)

3. **Severity guidelines validation:**
   - Verify all 6 severity levels are documented: `blocking`, `important`, `suggestion`, `nit`, `praise`, `learning`
   - Verify each severity has a clear description of when to use it
   - Verify each severity has a concrete example in valid JSON format
   - Parse each example JSON to ensure it's syntactically valid

4. **Complete example validation:**
   - Extract the complete example JSON from the template
   - Validate against `schema.json` (from Task #12): `npx ajv validate -s schema.json -d complete-example.json`
   - Copy the example to `.reviews/` directory with the documented filename
   - Load in VSCode with agent-review extension - verify it loads without errors
   - Verify comments appear at correct file/line locations (even if files don't exist, extension should handle gracefully)

5. **File naming convention accuracy:**
   - Verify documented patterns match regex in `src/parser.ts` `findReviewFiles()` function (lines 76-89)
   - Test example transformations manually:
     - `acme/backend-api` → `backend-api-pr-*-review-comments.json`
     - `acme/frontend_app` → `frontend-app-pr-*-review-comments.json`

6. **Tone guidelines usability:**
   - Verify each "Instead of / Write" pair demonstrates the questioning approach
   - Verify examples show constructive, non-commanding language

7. **AI agent integration test:**
   - Copy the entire content of `review-prompt.md` as a system prompt to an AI agent
   - Ask the agent to review a simple code snippet (provide mock PR details)
   - Verify the agent produces valid JSON output matching the documented schema
   - Verify the agent uses questioning tone as instructed
   - Verify severity levels are used appropriately

8. **Cross-reference with CLAUDE.md (Task #13):**
   - Ensure file naming conventions are consistent between both documents
   - Ensure schema documentation doesn't contradict CLAUDE.md
   - Ensure the prompt template can work standalone without requiring CLAUDE.md

tasks.title: Update README with AI Agent Workflow Documentation
tasks.description: Add a 'For AI Agents' section to README.md explaining how AI agents should generate review files, referencing schema.json, the prompt template, and documenting the new file naming convention with examples for multi-submodule repositories.
tasks.details:
Add a new "For AI Agents" section to `README.md` that consolidates documentation for AI agent integration.

**1. Add section after "Review JSON Schema" (around line 203):**

```markdown
## For AI Agents

This section provides instructions for AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) generating review files.

### Quick Reference

| Item | Value |
|------|-------|
| File location | `.reviews/` directory at workspace root |
| File naming | `<repo-slug>-pr-<number>-review-comments.json` |
| Schema | Reference `/schema.json` for JSON Schema 2020-12 validation |
| Prompt template | See `.taskmaster/templates/review-prompt.md` for detailed instructions |

### Generating a Review File

1. **Determine the filename** from `pr.repo`:
   - Extract repo name (part after `/`)
   - Convert to lowercase
   - Replace special characters with hyphens
   - Append `-pr-<number>-review-comments.json`

2. **Save to `.reviews/` directory** at the workspace root

3. **Reference the schema** for IDE autocomplete:
   ```json
   {
     "$schema": "./schema.json",
     ...
   }
   ```

### File Naming Convention

The filename is derived from `pr.repo` using this transformation:

| `pr.repo` value | Generated filename |
|-----------------|-------------------|
| `acme/backend-api` | `backend-api-pr-142-review-comments.json` |
| `acme/frontend_app` | `frontend-app-pr-58-review-comments.json` |
| `org/my.service.name` | `my-service-name-pr-99-review-comments.json` |

**Legacy format:** Files named `pr-<number>-review-comments.json` are still supported for backwards compatibility.
```

**2. Add multi-repo/submodule examples:**

```markdown
### Multi-Repo Workspaces and Monorepos

For monorepos with multiple submodules, each having their own PRs, the repo-prefixed naming prevents collisions:

```
my-monorepo/
├── .reviews/
│   ├── auth-service-pr-58-review-comments.json   # PR #58 in acme/auth-service submodule
│   ├── payments-api-pr-58-review-comments.json   # PR #58 in acme/payments-api submodule
│   └── web-frontend-pr-123-review-comments.json  # PR #123 in acme/web-frontend submodule
├── services/
│   ├── auth/     # submodule: acme/auth-service
│   └── payments/ # submodule: acme/payments-api
└── apps/
    └── web/      # submodule: acme/web-frontend
```

The extension resolves comment paths via `.gitmodules`. For example:
- Comment with `path: "src/handlers/auth.ts"` in `auth-service-pr-58-review-comments.json`
- Resolves to: `services/auth/src/handlers/auth.ts` based on `.gitmodules` mapping
```

**3. Reference external documentation files:**

```markdown
### Additional Resources

- **`/schema.json`** — JSON Schema 2020-12 definition for review files. Use for validation and IDE autocomplete.
- **`/.taskmaster/templates/review-prompt.md`** — Comprehensive prompt template with severity guidelines, tone recommendations, and complete examples.
- **`/CLAUDE.md`** — Machine-readable instructions for AI agents with minimal and full templates.
```

**4. Add minimal template inline for quick reference:**

```markdown
### Minimal Valid Review

```json
{
  "pr": {
    "repo": "org/repo-name",
    "number": 123,
    "title": "PR title",
    "base": "main",
    "head": "feature-branch"
  },
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "COMMENT",
    "overview": "Brief summary of the review.",
    "strengths": []
  },
  "comments": [
    {
      "path": "src/file.ts",
      "line": 42,
      "side": "RIGHT",
      "severity": "suggestion",
      "body": "Comment text in Markdown."
    }
  ]
}
```
```

**5. Update the existing "File naming" section (lines 68-73) to reference the new convention:**

Replace the current section with a pointer to the new detailed documentation or update it to show the new `<repo-slug>-pr-<number>` format as the primary convention while noting legacy support.

**6. Cross-reference placement:**

Add this section as a top-level heading after "Review JSON Schema" and before any appendix/troubleshooting sections. The structure should flow:
1. How it works (existing)
2. Setup (existing)
3. Usage (existing)
4. Review JSON Schema (existing)
5. **For AI Agents** (new section)

**7. Ensure consistency with related documentation:**

- File naming convention must match `findReviewFiles()` regex in `src/parser.ts` (lines 76-89)
- Schema reference must match Task #12's schema.json location
- Prompt template path must match Task #14's `.taskmaster/templates/review-prompt.md`
- CLAUDE.md reference must match Task #13's root-level CLAUDE.md
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Section existence and placement:**
   - Verify "For AI Agents" section exists in README.md after "Review JSON Schema"
   - Verify section uses proper markdown heading (##)
   - Verify table of contents (if present) includes the new section

2. **File naming documentation:**
   - Verify the documented pattern `<repo-slug>-pr-<number>-review-comments.json` is correct
   - Verify example transformations are accurate (test manually):
     - `acme/backend-api` → `backend-api-pr-142-review-comments.json`
     - `acme/frontend_app` → `frontend-app-pr-58-review-comments.json`
   - Verify legacy format `pr-<number>-review-comments.json` is mentioned

3. **Multi-repo example validation:**
   - Verify the monorepo directory structure example is clear
   - Verify path resolution explanation matches `src/resolver.ts` behavior
   - Verify `.gitmodules` reference is accurate

4. **Schema reference accuracy:**
   - Verify `/schema.json` path matches actual schema file location (from Task #12)
   - Verify schema version (JSON Schema 2020-12) is correct
   - Test that `$schema` example is syntactically valid JSON

5. **Prompt template reference:**

   - Verify the referenced file will exist after Task #14 completion

6. **CLAUDE.md reference:**
   - Verify `/CLAUDE.md` path is correct (root-level, from Task #13)
   - Verify the referenced file will exist after Task #13 completion

7. **Minimal template validation:**
   - Extract the minimal template JSON from README
   - Validate it's syntactically valid JSON
   - Create the file in `.reviews/` with appropriate name
   - Load in VSCode with agent-review extension

8. **Cross-reference consistency:**
   - Compare file naming convention with `src/parser.ts` `findReviewFiles()` regex

9. **Markdown rendering:**

   - Verify links and references are properly formatted

10. **AI agent usability test:**

    - Verify the output follows the documented conventions
    - Verify the filename is correctly derived from `pr.repo`

tasks.title: Prepare Repository for Open Source Release
tasks.description: Make the repository ready for public open-source release on GitHub, including license, contributing guidelines, issue/PR templates, CI setup, and cleanup of any internal references.
tasks.details:
Prepare the repository for open-source release:

1. **Add LICENSE file** — choose an appropriate open-source license (MIT recommended for VSCode extensions) and add the LICENSE file at the repo root.

2. **Add CONTRIBUTING.md** — document how external contributors can:
   - Set up the development environment
   - Run and test changes (F5 in VSCode)
   - Submit pull requests
   - Report issues

3. **Add GitHub issue and PR templates**:
   - `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md` — structured bug report template
   - `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md` — feature request template
   - `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` — PR checklist template

4. **Add CI workflow** (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`):
   - Run `npm run compile` on push/PR to verify TypeScript builds
   - Consider adding a lint step if ESLint is added later

5. **Update package.json**:
   - Add `repository` field pointing to the GitHub repo URL
   - Add `license` field (e.g., `MIT`)
   - Add `bugs` and `homepage` fields
   - Verify `publisher` is correct for marketplace publishing

6. **Review and clean up**:
   - Ensure no internal/private references remain in code or docs
   - Ensure `.gitignore` covers sensitive files (.env, credentials)
   - Ensure `.vscodeignore` is complete for clean VSIX packages
   - Remove or clean up `.taskmaster/` if not intended for public consumption
   - Review README.md for clarity from an external contributor's perspective

7. **Add CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md** — adopt Contributor Covenant or similar

8. **Create initial GitHub release** — tag v0.1.0, attach built .vsix as release asset
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify LICENSE file exists and contains correct license text
2. Verify CONTRIBUTING.md has setup, testing, and PR submission instructions
3. Verify issue templates render correctly on GitHub (create test issue)
4. Verify CI workflow runs successfully on push
5. Verify `npm run compile` and `npm run package` work from a clean clone
6. Grep for internal references (internal URLs, private package registries, etc.)
7. Verify .gitignore covers .env, node_modules, out/, *.vsix
8. Test fresh clone → npm install → npm run compile → F5 workflow

tasks.title: Support Syncing Individual Comments to GitHub
tasks.description: Add the ability to sync individual review comments to GitHub PRs, allowing users to post selected comments one at a time rather than the entire review batch.
tasks.testStrategy:
**Manual Testing Steps:**

1. **Basic Sync Workflow:**
   - Load a review file with multiple comments
   - Right-click on a comment in the diff view
   - Verify "Sync to GitHub" icon appears in the comment title bar
   - Click the sync button
   - Verify progress notification appears
   - Verify success message shows PR number
   - Check GitHub PR - verify the single comment appears with severity prefix
   - Verify the comment's label in VSCode now shows a checkmark (✓) indicating synced status

2. **Duplicate Prevention:**
   - Try to sync the same comment again
   - Verify warning dialog appears asking to confirm re-posting
   - Click "No" - verify no new comment is posted
   - Click "Yes" - verify a new comment is posted to GitHub

3. **Persistence Verification:**
   - After syncing a comment, close and reopen VSCode
   - Verify the synced comment still shows the checkmark indicator
   - Open the review JSON file and verify `githubCommentId` field exists on the synced comment

4. **Error Handling:**
   - Disconnect from internet or revoke `gh` CLI auth
   - Try to sync a comment
   - Verify appropriate error message is shown
   - Verify the review file is not corrupted

5. **Multi-Review Scenario:**
   - Load multiple review files for different PRs
   - Sync a comment from each review
   - Verify each comment posts to the correct PR on GitHub

6. **Edge Cases:**
   - Test with comments on different sides (LEFT/RIGHT)
   - Test with comments containing special characters (quotes, backticks, newlines)
   - Test with very long comment bodies
   - Test when gh CLI is not installed (should show clear error)

tasks.title: Create PR Overview Tree View in Activity Bar
tasks.description: Add a new Tree View in the VSCode Activity Bar with two sections: (1) 'Reviewed' — all PRs reviewed in this workspace grouped by repo, showing verdict icons and severity counts, and (2) 'Pending Review' — PRs where user is a requested reviewer, fetched via gh CLI. Include auto-refresh, error handling, and toolbar refresh button.
tasks.testStrategy:
**Manual Testing Steps:**

1. **Activity Bar Icon:**
   - After installing extension, verify new "Agent Reviews" icon appears in Activity Bar
   - Click icon to open the PR Overview sidebar

2. **Section Headers:**
   - Verify two sections appear: "Reviewed" and "Pending Review"
   - Both sections should be expanded by default

3. **Empty State:**
   - With no review files in `.reviews/` and no pending PRs, verify welcome message appears
   - Verify "Load Review File" button in welcome message works

4. **Reviewed Section - Single Repo:**
   - Place one review file in `.reviews/`
   - Verify repo appears as collapsible parent node (even with single PR)
   - Expand repo - verify PR appears with title, verdict icon, and severity counts
   - Verify severity counts format: "2 blocking · 3 important · 5 nit"
   - Click PR item - verify first commented file opens in diff view

5. **Reviewed Section - Multi Repo:**
   - Place review files from multiple repos in `.reviews/`
   - Verify each repo appears as separate collapsible node
   - Verify PRs grouped correctly under their repos

6. **Verdict Icons:**
   - Test PR with APPROVE verdict - verify checkmark icon
   - Test PR with REQUEST_CHANGES verdict - verify X icon
   - Test PR with COMMENT verdict - verify comment icon

7. **Pending Review Section:**
   - Ensure you have PRs awaiting your review on GitHub
   - Verify "Pending Review" section shows grouped by target repo
   - Verify each PR shows: number, title, author, and age (e.g., "3 days ago")
   - Click pending PR - verify nothing happens (placeholder for future)

8. **Refresh Button:**
   - Verify refresh button appears in view toolbar
   - Click refresh - verify both sections update
   - Verify loading indicator appears during fetch

9. **Auto-refresh:**
   - Keep panel open for 15+ minutes
   - Verify pending PRs refresh automatically
   - Close panel, wait, reopen - verify immediate refresh

10. **Error State:**
    - Disconnect from internet or break gh CLI auth
    - Verify error message appears in Pending Review section
    - Verify "Click to retry" option appears
    - Click retry - verify refresh is attempted

11. **Edge Cases:**
    - Test with review file having 0 comments - verify severity counts show nothing
    - Test with no pending review requests - verify section shows empty or message
    - Test with very long PR titles - verify truncation/tooltip

subtasks.title: Create PROverviewProvider with two-section tree structure
subtasks.description: Create src/prOverview.ts with PROverviewProvider implementing TreeDataProvider with 'Reviewed' and 'Pending Review' sections
subtasks.details: Define TreeItemType including 'section', 'repo', 'pr', PendingPR interface, PROverviewItem class extending vscode.TreeItem, and PROverviewProvider class with state for reviews, pendingPRs, pendingError, and isLoading. Implement getSections() returning two section header items, getTreeItem(), and getChildren() dispatching to appropriate methods based on element type.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify file compiles. Instantiate provider and call getChildren() with undefined to see two section items returned.

subtasks.title: Implement Reviewed section tree items grouped by repo
subtasks.description: Implement getReviewedRepoItems() and getReviewedPRsForRepo() methods to show reviewed PRs grouped by repository
subtasks.details: Group reviews by pr.repo field. Create repo nodes with $(repo) icon and always collapsible state. PR nodes show 'PR #N: Title', verdict icon ($(check)/$(x)/$(comment)), and severity count description using formatSeverityCounts() helper. Add click command to open first commented file diff.
subtasks.testStrategy: Load review files from multiple repos, verify repos appear as parent nodes, PRs nested correctly with proper icons and severity counts.

subtasks.title: Implement fetchPendingReviewPRs function
subtasks.description: Add function to fetch PRs where current user is a requested reviewer via gh CLI
subtasks.details: Use 'gh search prs --review-requested=@me --state=open --json repository,number,title,author,createdAt' command. Parse response into PendingPR[] array with repo as nameWithOwner. Handle command errors gracefully.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify function returns array of PendingPR objects with correct fields when gh CLI is authenticated and user has pending reviews.

subtasks.title: Implement Pending Review section tree items with age formatting
subtasks.description: Implement getPendingRepoItems() and getPendingPRsForRepo() methods to show pending review PRs grouped by target repo
subtasks.details: Group pending PRs by repo field. Create repo nodes similar to Reviewed section. PR nodes show 'PR #N: Title' with description 'author • 3 days ago' using formatAge() helper. No click command (placeholder for future). Add $(git-pull-request) icon.
subtasks.testStrategy: Authenticate gh CLI with user having pending reviews. Verify pending PRs appear grouped by repo with correct age formatting.

subtasks.title: Register TreeView and viewsContainer in package.json
subtasks.description: Add viewsContainers, views, and viewsWelcome configuration to package.json contributes
subtasks.details: Add 'agent-reviews' activitybar container with $(comment-discussion) icon. Register 'agentReview.prOverview' view. Add viewsWelcome content for empty state with Load Review File button.
subtasks.testStrategy: After adding config, verify Activity Bar shows new icon. Open panel with no reviews to see welcome message.

subtasks.title: Register TreeView and refresh command in extension.ts
subtasks.description: Wire up PROverviewProvider in activate(), create TreeView with visibility tracking, and register refresh command
subtasks.details: Use vscode.window.createTreeView() with showCollapseAll option. Track visibility with onDidChangeVisibility event. Register 'agentReview.refreshPROverview' command. Update provider with allReviews after loadAllReviews() completes. Add refresh button to view/title menu in package.json.
subtasks.testStrategy: Open panel, verify refresh button in toolbar. Click refresh and verify both sections update.

subtasks.title: Implement auto-refresh with 15-minute interval
subtasks.description: Add auto-refresh logic that refreshes pending PRs on panel open and every 15 minutes while visible
subtasks.details: Use setInterval with 15 * 60 * 1000ms. Start interval in onDidChangeVisibility when visible=true, stop when visible=false. Call refreshPendingPRs() immediately on panel open. Set loading state during fetch. Clean up interval in extension deactivate.
subtasks.testStrategy: Open panel, verify immediate fetch. Keep open 15+ minutes and verify auto-refresh. Close and reopen to verify immediate refresh on reopen.

subtasks.title: Implement error state with retry for pending review fetch
subtasks.description: Handle gh CLI fetch failures with error message and retry option in tree view
subtasks.details: When fetchPendingReviewPRs throws, call setPendingError(). In getPendingRepoItems(), if pendingError is set, return error item and retry item with command pointing to agentReview.refreshPROverview. Clear error on successful fetch.
subtasks.testStrategy: Break gh CLI auth or disconnect network. Verify error message appears. Click retry item and verify fetch is reattempted.
